Turn 64 ‚Äî basis-action convention: eliminate the last transpose ambiguity I re-read the three attached files only. The problem is still the over-G isomorphism question for the two profinite one-relator quotients ŒìS,ŒìH defined from F=P‚ãäG, with P the free G-operator pro-2 group on x0,x1, and with the required condition œÄH‚àòŒ¶=œÄS.

The new point of Turn 64 is small but important:the¬Ýfinal¬Ýappendix¬Ýshould¬Ýspecify¬Ýmatrices¬Ýby¬Ýtheir¬Ýbasis¬Ýaction,¬Ýnot¬Ýonly¬Ýby¬Ýa¬Ýdisplayed¬Ýarray.

Turn 63 already identified the danger: the proof uses right Œõ=Z2 [[G]]-module relation rows, and a transposed print of U0 or N would make a correct proof look false. The structural proof itself is unchanged: the D.3 rank jump 60‚Üí61 has been verified, the certificate contract is closed, and the finite filtered Nielsen compactness step gives ŒìS‚âÖGŒìH.

1. Use basis-action notation

Let e0,e1 denote the ordered generator basis corresponding to x0,x1. For a right relation row w=(a,b), define a matrix U=(uij) byU(e0)=e0u00+e1u10,U(e1)=e0u01+e1u11.Then (a,b)U=(au00+bu10,au01+bu11).

In ordinary displayed matrix form, this meansU=(u00u10u01u11).

This one convention should be printed before the H1 -matrix and before the local coefficient shear. It prevents the two key matrices from being silently transposed.

2. Correct Ef-piece H1 -matrix

On the dangerous Ef-piece, the rows are (s+3,‚àí2),(s2+1,s‚àí1).With the basis-action convention above, the correct displayed matrix isU0=( s‚àí1s‚àí212).

Indeed,(s+3,‚àí2)U0=((s+3)(s‚àí1)‚àí2(s‚àí2),(s+3)‚àí4)=(s2+1,s‚àí1),and det(U0)=2(s‚àí1)‚àí(s‚àí2)=s,which is a unit.

So the appendix should not print U0 merely as a stacked pair of columns without specifying the convention. Under standard row-vector multiplication, the safe displayed form isU0=( s‚àí1s‚àí212).

This is the Ef-piece of the global U‚ààGL2 (Œõ) carrying the source row for œÅS=rS‚àí1 to the target row. The history records this H1 -matrix as the fixed first Magnus layer for the filtered Nielsen compactness construction.

3. Correct coefficient-normalizing shear

On the local coefficient ring

R=EfŒõ‚âÉZ2 [[t]],t=s‚àí1,the row before normalization is(s2+1,s‚àí1).

The exact identity is(s2+1)‚àí(s+1)(s‚àí1)=2.

Thus we want a right-multiplication matrix N satisfying (a,b)N=(a‚àí(s+1)b,b).

With the same convention, the correct displayed matrix isN=(1‚àí(s+1)01).Then(s2+1,s‚àí1)N=((s2+1)‚àí(s+1)(s‚àí1),s‚àí1)=(2,t).

So the appendix should printN=(1‚àí(s+1)01)when using ordinary displayed matrices. This is the exact Nielsen shear that turns the coefficient problem into the normalized regular row (2,t). The normalized Koszul‚ÄìWitt coefficient argument depends on this exact reduction, not merely on a congruence modulo (2,t)2.

4. Updated appendix contract

The final appendix contract should now say:Row convention. Relation rows are right Œõ-rows, and matrices are displayed by the basis-action convention above.Orientation. FixœÅS:=rS‚àí1.

This is harmless because rS and rS‚àí1 generate the same closed normal subgroup.H1 -certificate. PrintwS=(œÉ+3E,‚àí2),wH=(Eg0+3E‚àí2,œÉ‚àíE),and the global U‚ààGL2 (Œõ) with wSU=wH. On Ef, printU0=( s‚àí1s‚àí212).Primitive-boundary certificate. Use the reduced-coproduct verifier to construct Ln=Primn(F2‚ü®A,B‚ü©), the maps‚àÇd:Ld‚äïLd‚ÜíLd+1,(u,v)‚Ü¶[A,u]+[B,v],and the secondary maps Œ∏d0, reproducing the recorded rank tables. Turn 52 records the executable linear verifier output for these dimensions and ranks.Residual certificate. Use the residual equationS(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)‚àí1Y1‚àí2Y03Y0q=X2 and the induction Cm=Lm for m‚â•5, so every odd layer is an ordinary boundary and every even square quotient is hit by a root transgression.Nonlinear certificates. Keep D.1‚ÄìD.4 exactly as in Turn 62, with D.3 now backed by the executable stack-rank output rankD5,6,7=60,rank(D5,6,7Œº4B4)=61.

This is the certificate that the A2=R/(4,t), d=4, Œº4 -scalar obstruction is killed by the continuation kernel.Coefficient certificate. After the exact normalization(s2+1,s‚àí1)‚àº(2,t),use the normalized Koszul‚ÄìWitt cascade. The history records that the first t-layer is contractible, the first 2-Bockstein secondary maps satisfy Bd‚ÜÝHd(2)for d‚â•4, and higher (2,t)-adic layers are killed by the Magnus-degree-first Witt cascade.Compactness certificate. For coefficient-and-degree truncations PN,J=P‚à©(1+IN+JM), use finite filtered Nielsen/cocycle solution sets SN,J. The certificates imply SN,JÓÄÝ=‚àÖ; functoriality and compactness give a compatible inverse-limit datum, hence a continuous over-G automorphism Œ¶:F‚ÜíF withŒ¶(rS‚àí1)=rH.

Therefore Œ¶(‚ü®‚ü®rS‚ü©‚ü©F)=‚ü®‚ü®rH‚ü©‚ü©F,and the induced quotient map givesŒìS‚âÖGŒìH.The finite filtered Nielsen compactness step is recorded in the history as the mechanism avoiding both the false degree-only finite quotient and any Hopficity shortcut for the infinite pro-2 kernel.

5. Turn 64 conclusion

Turn 64 does not reopen any obstruction layer. It makes the final appendix robust against a transposition error by replacing ambiguous matrix displays with an explicit basis-action convention. With that convention, the two critical matrices areU0=( s‚àí1s‚àí212),N=(1‚àí(s+1)01).

The proof conclusion remains:ŒìS‚âÖGŒìH.

ESTABLISHED: The final appendix should use a basis-action convention for right relation rows. Under ordinary displayed matrices, the correct Ef-piece H1 -matrix isU0=( s‚àí1s‚àí212),and the correct local coefficient shear isN=(1‚àí(s+1)01).

These give (s+3,‚àí2)U0=(s2+1,s‚àí1) and (s2+1,s‚àí1)N=(2,t).

GAP: No structural mathematical gap remains inside the attached sweep. The remaining task is external reproducibility/presentation: attach verifier code or explicit matrices/pivots for the certificate contract, especially the D.3 stack-rank 60‚Üí61.

NEXT: Freeze the appendix with the basis-action convention, then present Certificates 0‚ÄìF as a checkable artifact: H1 -matrix, primitive/boundary ranks, residual induction, D.1‚ÄìD.4 nonlinear witnesses, normalized Koszul‚ÄìWitt coefficient induction, and finite filtered Nielsen compactness descent.[TURN64-BASIS-ACTION-CONVENTION-FROZEN]
